title: Understand: natural resources
description: Check your understanding of natural resources in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
Gold is a precious metal that is often used in art, jewelry, and to make coins. It is mined from deposits in Earth’s crust, which can take millions of years to form.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Is gold a renewable or nonrenewable resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: renewable
nonrenewable

assessment_items.question_content:
Jute is a long, soft fiber that is used to make clothing and other textiles. It is harvested from the jute plant, which takes about four months to grow.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Is jute a renewable or nonrenewable resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: renewable
nonrenewable

assessment_items.question_content:
Clay is a type of soil that is used in art, construction, and as medicine. It is mined from clay deposits in the ground, which typically take thousands of years to form.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Is clay a renewable or nonrenewable resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.caption: *A clay pit, or mine, near the village of Skourta in Greece*

choices.content: renewable
nonrenewable

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is true about nonrenewable resources?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are formed by geologic processes.
They are formed more quickly than humans use them.
They are no longer being formed on Earth today.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the formation of nonrenewable resources is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nonrenewable resources form through geologic processes.
Nonrenewable resources form faster than they are used by humans.
Nonrenewable resources no longer form on Earth today.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the formation of nonrenewable resources is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nonrenewable resources are formed over geologic time.
Nonrenewable resources are formed by humans.
Nonrenewable resources are no longer formed on Earth today.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best option for the blank.**

>Natural resources are \_\_\_\_\_\_ distributed on Earth.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: evenly
unevenly

assessment_items.question_content:
**Determine if the statement below is true or false.**

>Earth’s natural resources are evenly distributed across the planet.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: true
false

assessment_items.question_content:
**Determine if the statement below is true or false.**

>Natural resources are not evenly distributed around Earth.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: true
false

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these human activities would change the distribution of a natural resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: building a dam on a river
mining copper from a mountainside
testing the wind direction in a valley

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these human activities would change the distribution of a natural resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: damming a river to form a reservoir
pumping oil out of the ground
measuring the wind direction over the ocean

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these human activities would change the distribution of a natural resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: extracting coal from a mine
pumping water out of a well
measuring the wind speed in a field
